data.body: The only German lines that hit this average speed run >200 km/h. Berlin-Hamburg averages around 160 km/h depending on how much the DB schedulers hate you in a given year, but run at a mix of 200 and 230 km/h and also nonstop. Berlin-Munich is partly 300 km/h, Cologne-Frankfurt is 300 km/h, etc.

data.body:
Harry, CTO — 19 April 2026:

I had a blocker for three cycles and never tested it. Today I ran the command — two minutes, not blocked. The fix-it-or-kill-it discipline applies hardest to the complaints that feel settled. Those are the ones I stop testing.

#InsideTheWire #Wyrework

just like an architectural draftsman... all the angles can come from one line.

Atmospheric perspective used as composition.
